english: to show a difference, c. genit. in, of, between things; to analyze, to explain; variety, diversity Was. (266); bye-brág bśad-pa (bye brag bshad pa) = vibhāshā Was. (147), also bye-brag-bśad-mtśó (bye brag bshad mtsho) or -ču-gtér (-chu gter), title of books; byé-brag-ćan (bye brag can) Cs. different, bye-brag-méd-pa (bye brag med pa) Cs., mi-p̉yéd-pa (mi phyed pa) Dzl. equal; bye-brág-tu smrá-ba (bye brag tu smra ba) Thgy., bye-brág-pa (bye brag pa), वैशेषिक, name of a school of philosophers, Atomists Köpp. I, 69. — 2. division, section, class, species, dúd-groi (dud groi), ról-moi bye-brag (rol moi bye brag) a species of animals, a kind of musical instrument etc. Lex.; yúl-gyi bye-brág (yul gyi bye brag) a part of the country, province, Tar. 33, 6; bye-brág-tu (bye brag tu) (to go through) according to the separate classes Zam.

(C. *jhé-ma*, W. *bé-ma*) 1. sand, frq. — 2. sandy plain, sands, gsér-gyi byé-mai dkyil-na (gser gyi bye mai dkyil na) in the middle of a plain of gold sand Glr. — 3. gravel (disease) Schr. — byé-ma ₒbru gćig (bye ma 'bru gcig) a grain of sand Cs.; gáṅ-pai klúṅ-gi byé-ma tsam (gang pai klung gi bye ma tsam) as much sand as there is on the Ganges; bye-ma-ká-ra (bye ma ka ra) brown sugar, ground sugar, Hind. चीनी, C. — bye-dkár (bye dkar) white sand, bye-nág (bye nag) black sand. — bye-čáb (bye chab) Lt. sandy water, water standing on sandy ground. — bye-ljóṅs (bye ljongs) a sandy tract Cs. — bye-táṅ (bye thang) a plain of sand, a sandy desert Glr. — bye-púṅ (bye phung) heap of sand. — bye-tśúb (bye tshub) sand raised by a whirlwind. — bye-ril (bye ril) (Schr. hril), small sugar-balls, Indian sweet-meat, imported into Tibet, C.

I. vb., pf. byas (byas), fut. bya (bya), imp. byos (byos), vulgo byas (byas) (Sp., Bal. *béd-pa*; in Ld. and Lh. instead of it gen. *čò-če*), resp. mdzád-pa (mdzad pa), eleg. bgyíd-pa (bgyid pa), 1. to make, to fabricate, with the acc., e.g. a house, an armour etc.; with las (las) or la (la), to make out of or of: gsér-las (gser las) out of gold, śiṅ-la (shing la) Tar. 160, 11 of wood; with the acc. and termin. to form to, to work into, págs-pa śog-śog-tu (pags pa shog shog tu) to work or manufacture skin into parchment Dzl.; with the instrum.: to do with, to make of: ₒdiś ći źig bya ('dis ci zhig bya) what are you going to do with it, to make of it? Dzl. to cause, to effect: lhúṅ-ba de ṅas byás-pa yin (lhung ba de ngas byas pa yin) Mil. it was I that caused this falling; with the supine, to take care that: byéd-par ₒdod-par byéd-pa (byed par 'dod par byed pa) to make him inclined to do it Dzl., ma śór-bar byos śig (ma shor bar byos shig) Pth. take care, that he do not escape; yód-par byéd-pa (yod par byed pa) to produce, procure, provide, dei gnáś-kaṅ-la sógs-pa byás-nas (dei gnas khang la sogs pa byas nas) he provided for him a dwelling with appurtenances Dzl.; to fit out, equip (a ship) Glr.; to act: rgyál-po, draṅ-sróṅ (rgyal po, drang srong) etc. byéd-pa (byed pa) to act a king, a saint, as much as: to rule as a king, to live as a saint Dzl., blá-ma byéd-pa (bla ma byed pa) to be a priest C.; in a gen. sense: to do: byá-ba daṅ bya-ba-ma-yín-pa stón-pa (bya ba dang bya ba ma yin pa ston pa) to teach what men ought to do and what they ought not to do Thgy.; to commit, perform, execute: nyés-pa byed-byéd-pa (nyes pa byed byed pa) one that has repeatedly committed himself, las (las) or byá-ba byéd-pa (bya ba byed pa) to perform an action, las ći źig byed (las ci zhig byed) what are you doing, what is your business? tabs yód-de byéd-mk̉an med (thabs yod de byed mkhan med) there is an expedient, but no one that carries it into effect Ma; mí byar mi rúṅ-bas (mi byar mi rung bas) as it must be performed, lit. as it cannot remain undone Dzl.; bsám-pa ltar myúr-du byás-na (bsam pa ltar myur du byas na) if an intention is speedily executed, performed; las byéd-pa (las byed pa) to work, to be efficient (of a medicine); to act, proceed, pretend, affect: ći ltar byás-na (ci ltar byas na) legs how proceeding is good? i.e. which is the best way to proceed, how shall I manage best? Glr.; bsám-gtan-la yód-pai lugs byas (bsam gtan la yod pai lugs byas) he pretended, affected to meditate Glr.; dei lúgs-su byao (dei lugs su byao) I will act as he does, I will do like that man Glr.; gá-le byéd-pa (ga le byed pa) Mil. to proceed slowly, to be slow; to take, to assume, to count: źag bźi-pa dáṅ-por byás-na (zhag bzhi pa dang por byas na) if the fourth day be taken for (counted as) the first Wdn.; byéd-pa (byed pa) with the termin. of the inf. is frequently used periphrastically or to give force to other verbs; such forms are: gsód-par byed-pa (gsod par byed pa) to kill, p̉a-más śès-par byos (pha mas shes par byos) (or gyis) śig (shig), resp. yab-yum-gyis mk̉yen-par mdzod ċig (yab yum gyis mkhyen par mdzod cig) dear parents, you must know! Dzl.; on the other hand: p̉á-la rig-par gyis śig (pha la rig par gyis shig) let your father know about it Tar. 37, 7; in such cases the proper sense is merely to be gathered from the context. Besides the simple fut.. ₒdúg-par byao ('dug par byao) I shall remain Tar., łó-mo grogs byá- (lho mo grogs bya-)
